overview.description: Imprint is a financial technology company that partners with brands to design, configure, and operate co-branded credit card programs and "buy now, pay later" solutions. The company's platform focuses on embedded, white-labeled financial products that give brands greater control over customer relationships, payment data, and loyalty economics compared to traditional co-brand issuers.
overview.headquarters: New York, NY (with additional office in Bellevue, WA)
overview.employeeCount: 150-200 (estimated from LinkedIn's 51-200 range and 233 associated members)
overview.mission: To build the easiest and most rewarding way to pay and transform co-brand cards into a true loyalty platform

timeline.title: Company Founded
timeline.description: Imprint founded by Daragh Murphy (Co Kildare, Ireland native and Duke University graduate) to address the need for modern co-branded credit card technology

timeline.title: Series A Funding
timeline.description: Raised $38M Series A led by Kleiner Perkins with participation from Stripe

timeline.title: Series C Funding
timeline.description: Raised $75M Series C led by Keith Rabois at Khosla Ventures, reaching $600M valuation

timeline.title: Major Partnership Wins
timeline.description: Secured partnerships with Rakuten, Booking.com, and other major brands

timeline.title: Fitch AAA Rating
timeline.description: Achieved AAA investment rating from Fitch Ratings for inaugural $300M securitization

timeline.title: Unicorn Status
timeline.description: Raised $150M Series D at $1.2B valuation, achieving unicorn status

recentNews.title: Imprint Raises $150 Million Series D to Transform Co-Brand Cards into a True Loyalty Platform
recentNews.summary: Imprint secures $150M Series D at $1.2B valuation, achieving unicorn status as brands increasingly turn to the company to replace legacy bank-issued programs.

recentNews.title: Imprint hits unicorn status following $150m Series D
recentNews.summary: Imprint's latest raise doubled the company's valuation to $1.2 billion from $600 million, following strong growth with 200% year-over-year cardholder base increase.

recentNews.title: This Fintech Helps You Get More Credit Card Rewards At Retailers And On Vacation
recentNews.summary: Forbes profiles Imprint's cloud-based ledger system that allows retailers to micro-target rewards and its competition with traditional co-brand issuers like Synchrony and Capital One.

perception.overallSentiment: Imprint (Financial Services) faces mixed public perception with significant negative feedback around billing practices and customer service, while leadership maintains positive industry visibility through conferences and interviews.

redditDiscussions.title: Imprint is charging interest on a $0 previous statement after Crate
redditDiscussions.content: Filed complaints with BBB and consumer financial protection bureau, spent 45 minutes arguing on phone about improper interest charges

positive: Quick customer service response when reached

highlights: App Store: 4.8/5 stars but Google Play: 3.1/5
Trustpilot: 1.9/5 with complaints about trial billing
BBB complaints about system errors and continuous interest charges
Multiple reports of being charged after canceling free trials

pressCoverage.title: Talking with Daragh Murphy, founder and CEO of Imprint
pressCoverage.summary: CEO discusses company's co-branded credit card technology and future plans

videos.title: Daragh Murphy, CEO of Imprint - $200M to Disrupt Co-Branded Credit Cards
videos.speaker: Daragh Murphy, CEO
videos.description: Interview with Imprint CEO about the company's $200M funding and disruption in co-branded credit card space

videos.title: The Future of Investing in Entertainment | Imprint Debt Fund Launch
videos.description: Discussion about Imprint's entertainment debt fund and financing for entertainment projects

leadership.name: Daragh Murphy
leadership.title: CEO and Co-Founder
leadership.background: Previously experienced at WeWork, holds a JD from Duke University School of Law. Founded Imprint in 2020 and has led the company through multiple funding rounds totaling $200M+ from top-tier VCs.

leadership.name: Will Larson
leadership.title: Chief Technology Officer
leadership.background: Seasoned technology leader with 15+ years scaling engineering teams. Previously CTO at Carta and Calm, led engineering teams at Stripe and Uber. Author of three books on engineering management, holds BS in Computer Science from Centre College.

leadership.name: Mariana Pali Coontz
leadership.title: Chief Financial Officer and Chief Operating Officer
leadership.background: Joined in May 2023, bringing experience from JPMorgan Chase and Synchrony Financial. Oversees financial operations and strategy.

leadership.name: Sean Rowles
leadership.title: Chief Risk Officer
leadership.background: Former PayPal executive who joined in October 2023. Oversees credit policy, compliance, and regulatory alignment.

leadership.name: Melissa Davis Bar-Ziv
leadership.title: Chief Commercial Officer
leadership.background: Joined in January 2025, responsible for go-to-market strategy and partner relationships.

leadership.name: Livingston Miller
leadership.title: General Counsel and Chief Business Officer
leadership.background: Leads legal affairs and business operations for the company.

leadership.name: Colin Groshong
leadership.title: Chief Capital Officer & Business Officer
leadership.background: Oversees capital markets and business development initiatives.

leadership.name: Tyler Dibble
leadership.title: Chief Revenue Officer
leadership.background: Leads revenue generation and sales strategy.

people.teamSize: ~200 (based on LinkedIn showing 51-200 employees with 233 associated members)

hiringTrends.summary: Imprint is actively hiring across technical and business functions with 30+ open positions, signaling rapid scaling. Their focus on data science, strategic finance, and partnership roles suggests they're preparing for larger brand partnerships and more sophisticated financial products.
signals: Investing heavily in data and analytics capabilities
Preparing for next phase of growth post-Series D

cultureSignals.signal: High-impact, high-ownership atmosphere where collaboration thrives with knowledgeable leadership invested in employee development
Hiring philosophy prioritizes values alignment over just talent - looking for high tolerance for uncertainty, pride in work, drive to move forward, and low entitlement
Rejects 'cult of personality' leadership approach, focuses on mission and values-driven culture rather than following a single leader
Fast-moving, high-expectation culture that's not right for those who prefer slower pace or highly structured environment
Team described as 'incredibly sharp, thoughtful, and collaborative' with high bar but supportive environment
One negative review mentioned 'horrible company culture' though this appears to be an outlier
Focus on clarity, focus, speed, hard work paired with clear strategy and execution - trust built through humble team members
